title: Marek Meets Sara
description: Listen to Marek and Sara meet for the first time

exercises.conversationTranscriptSk: Marek: Ahoj! Volám sa Marek. Ako sa voláš? Sara: Ahoj Marek! Volám sa Sara. Teší ma. Marek: Mňa tiež. Odkiaľ si? Sara: Som z Talianska. Marek: Vitaj v Bratislave! Sara: Ďakujem! Si z Bratislavy? Marek: Nie. Som z Nitry, ale bývam tu v Bratislave. Sara: Rozumiem. A tvoja rodina, býva tu v Bratislave? Marek: Nie. Moji rodičia bývajú v Nitre. A ty? Sara: Nie, nemám rodinu tu. Moji rodičia bývajú v Taliansku. Ale bývam s mačkou. Marek: Ako sa volá tvoja mačka? Sara: Volá sa Luna. A ty, máš zviera? Marek: Áno, mám psa. Volá sa Rex. Luna je pekné meno! Sara: Ďakujem! Rex tiež. Som rada, že sme sa spoznali, Marek! Marek: Ja tiež, Sara! Som šťastný tu na Slovensku. Sara: Ja som šťastná tu tiež!

exercises.instruction: Tap every word or expression you heard in the conversation. (Part 1 of 2)
Tap every word or expression you heard in the conversation. (Part 2 of 2)

exercises.conversationTranscriptSk: Marek: Ahoj! Volám sa Marek. Ako sa voláš? Sara: Ahoj Marek! Volám sa Sara. Teší ma. Marek: Mňa tiež. Odkiaľ si? Sara: Som z Talianska. Marek: Vitaj v Bratislave! Sara: Ďakujem! Si z Bratislavy? Marek: Nie. Som z Nitry, ale bývam tu v Bratislave. Sara: Rozumiem. A tvoja rodina, býva tu v Bratislave? Marek: Nie. Moji rodičia bývajú v Nitre. A ty? Sara: Nie, nemám rodinu tu. Moji rodičia bývajú v Taliansku. Ale bývam s mačkou. Marek: Ako sa volá tvoja mačka? Sara: Volá sa Luna. A ty, máš zviera? Marek: Áno, mám psa. Volá sa Rex. Luna je pekné meno! Sara: Ďakujem! Rex tiež. Som rada, že sme sa spoznali, Marek! Marek: Ja tiež, Sara! Som šťastný tu na Slovensku. Sara: Ja som šťastná tu tiež!

questions.questionEn: How did Marek ask Sara her name?
questions.answerMeaning: Ako sa voláš? = What's your name? (informal Ty form). Marek used the informal form because they are peers — if speaking to a stranger in a formal context, you would say Ako sa voláte? (Vy form).

questions.questionEn: Where is Sara from and where does she live now?
questions.answerMeaning: Som z Talianska, ale bývam tu v Bratislave = I am from Italy, but I live here in Bratislava. Same Som z... / bývam v... pattern from Lessons 1 and 2.

reviewPair.slovak: Som z Talianska, ale bývam v Bratislave
reviewPair.english: I am from Italy but I live in Bratislava

questions.questionEn: What did Marek say when he found out Sara was new to Bratislava?
questions.answerMeaning: Vitaj v Bratislave! = Welcome to Bratislava! Vitaj is the informal welcome — you would say Vitajte for a more formal situation or a group of people.

questions.questionEn: Why doesn't Sara have family in Bratislava?

answers.textEn: She has no family at all

questions.answerMeaning: Nemám rodinu tu. Moji rodičia bývajú v Taliansku = I don't have family here. My parents live in Italy. Nemám = I don't have — the negative of Mám from the Verbs lesson.

questions.questionEn: Who asked about the other person's pet first?

answers.textEn: Neither asked — they both just mentioned it

questions.answerMeaning: Sara asked: Máš zviera? = Do you have a pet? Marek then revealed he has a dog called Rex. Máš = Do you have? — the Ty form question from Mám/Máš pattern.

questions.questionEn: What do Marek and Sara have in common by the end of the conversation?

answers.textEn: They both live in Bratislava and are happy in Slovakia

questions.answerMeaning: Both said they live in Bratislava and both expressed being happy: Som šťastný tu na Slovensku (Marek, male form) and Som šťastná tu tiež (Sara, female form). Notice the -ý vs -á ending difference based on gender.

reviewPair.slovak: Som šťastný / šťastná tu na Slovensku
reviewPair.english: I am happy here in Slovakia

sentences.promptEn: I don't have family here. I live with my cat.
